signals.reasoning: BTC is relatively stable, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH is too mixed to justify a fresh trade while the event brake is active.
SOL has some constructive structure, but veto keeps it out of the buy bucket for now.
TRB lacks enough clean edge or trade quality to justify action during the veto regime.
STBL remains a valid bearish fade because funding is still strongly positive and the market remains crowded enough on the long side to look fragile.
AVNT is too average on both edge and liquidity quality to justify action here.
FOGO is one of the cleaner bearish names because the board leans negative and the price action remains weak enough that continuation is more likely than reversal.
PEOPLE has a bearish lean and weak trade quality, which makes it a modest downside candidate rather than anything to chase long.
GRIFFAIN does not have enough structural edge to justify a trade in the current regime.
CHILLGUY remains extended and crowded, but not clean enough to trade under veto conditions.
TAO is the clearest bearish name on the board because trend is still decisively weak and the mechanical bias is firmly negative.
